channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: AI Agent stack you need Context, Auth, and Cognitive Debt
item.description: Most AI content teaches you how to write prompts. This is not that. I've spent three years at Ona building platform infrastructure for 1.7 million developers. I'm the first independent maintainer...

item.title: Scaling Flask for High-Concurrency I/O: From Thread Starvation to Async Mastery
item.description: Scaling Flask for High-Concurrency I/O: From Thread Starvation to Async Mastery The Queue That Never Empties You've built a solid Flask application. It works great for your typical...

item.title: 🚨 “Everything Was Green… But Production Was Broken” — A Debugging Story Every Backend Engineer Needs
item.description: 0 errors. 0 alerts. 100% failure. At 2 AM, everything in our dashboards was green. No spikes 📊 No errors ❌ No alerts 🚨 And yet… 👉 Orders were failing 👉 Inventory was stuck 👉 Business impact was...

item.title: Flutter MVP in 8 Weeks? Yes, With AI Agents.
item.description: This article was originally published on BuildZn. Everyone talks about launching fast, but nobody explains how to really do it without burning cash or compromising quality. Founders constantly get...

item.title: Most Developers Are Using AI Wrong, And It Shows
item.description: You can see it in the output. Projects that look complete, but fall apart under real usage. Code that works, but no one can explain why. Systems that ship fast, but degrade even faster. AI didn’t...

item.title: Implementing Self-Service TLS in Kubernetes Post-Ingress-Nginx: A Gateway API v1.5 Solution
item.description: Introduction The retirement of ingress-nginx has created a critical gap in Kubernetes ecosystems, particularly in self-service TLS management. Ingress-nginx, historically relied upon for its...

item.title: An Internet of Lies
item.description: The digital world, once hailed as a liberating force for information and a catalyst for global connection, now stands at a perilous crossroads. We inhabit an internet where the lines between fact...

item.title: How I Load Test a PDF Generation API with k6, Docker, and GitHub Actions
item.description: The Problem with "It Works on My Machine" PDF generation is one of those deceptively expensive operations. You fire off a request, Puppeteer spins up a headless Chromium, renders a full HTML page,...

item.title: Digital Monasticism
item.description: A New Form of Retreat As the Roman Empire expanded, with its complex bureaucracy and sprawling cities, some early Christians retreated into the desert to seek a simpler, more direct connection...

item.title: LiteLLM vs Bifrost: Why the Supply Chain Attack Changes Everything for LLM Gateways
item.description: If you're running LiteLLM in production, the March 2026 supply chain attack probably got your attention. Mine too. I spent the past few days digging into what happened, why it happened, and what...

item.title: Sony temporarily suspends memory card sales due to shortages
item.description: Sony has announced that as of March 27th, 2026, the company is no longer accepting orders for nearly all the products in its CFexpress and SD memory card lines. The list of affected memory...

item.title: STADLER reshapes knowledge work at a 230-year-old company
item.description: Learn how STADLER uses ChatGPT to transform knowledge work, saving time and accelerating productivity across 650 employees.

item.title: AI Research Is Getting Harder to Separate From Geopolitics
item.description: A policy change announced by NeurIPS, the world’s leading AI research conference, drew widespread backlash from Chinese researchers this week and then was quickly reversed.

item.title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
item.description: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

item.title: Insta360’s last-gen Link 2C is fantastic 4K webcam now that it’s 20 percent off
item.description: If you spend a lot of time presenting or chatting over video, you know how big a difference a decent webcam can make. Thankfully, if you’re looking to level up your setup at home or at work, the...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Bioluminescent Bacteria in Squid
item.description: The Hawaiian bobtail squid has bioluminescent bacteria.

item.title: The White House has an app now, and Trump wants you to report people to ICE on it
item.description: A new official White House app on Android and iOS takes the content from the White House website and copies it into app format. A tweet announcing the app on Friday morning appeared alongside a...

item.title: Wait, the Trump phone might actually exist
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This week I think I've found an FCC listing for...

item.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
item.description: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.

item.title: The latest in data centers, AI, and energy
item.description: Massive new data centers are the physical foundation for tech companies’ hopes and dreams for AI. But the rush to expand warehouses full of energy-hungry servers has also kicked up fights across...

item.title: Anker’s 160W Prime Charger can power three devices at once, and it’s $50 off
item.description: If you’re constantly switching between working at home and on the go, Anker’s latest 160W Prime Charger can lighten your load by replacing multiple chargers with a single device. It’s normally...

item.title: Apple Sends Lock Screen Alerts to Outdated iPhones Over Active Web-Based Exploits
item.description: Apple is now sending Lock Screen notifications to iPhones and iPads running older versions of iOS and iPadOS to alert users of web-based attacks and urge them to install the update. The...

item.title: TeamPCP Pushes Malicious Telnyx Versions to PyPI, Hides Stealer in WAV Files
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the supply chain attack targeting Trivy, KICS, and litellm, has now compromised the telnyx Python package by pushing two malicious versions to steal sensitive...

item.title: Nuki’s one-touch retrofit smart lock got its first-ever discount
item.description: The Nuki Smart Lock is the best retrofit smart lock currently available for people who want a minimalist, feature-packed upgrade to their preexisting deadlock. It’s currently selling for $149 at...

item.title: VCs are betting billions on AI’s next wave, so why is OpenAI killing Sora?
item.description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres...

item.title: This modular crafting machine can create custom shirts, phone cases, and molds
item.description: xTool, a company best known amongst makers for its laser-based cutting and engraving tools, has announced a new multi-function crafting machine called the WonderPress. It's not an all-in-one...

item.title: Apple Still Plans to Sell iPhones When It Turns 100
item.description: As the tech giant turns 50, WIRED spoke to executives about how they plan to win in the AI era.

item.title: We handpicked the 24 best Big Spring Sale deals under $50
item.description: Despite the fact that spring isn’t often associated with big discounts, Amazon seems keen to turn every season into one filled with savings. Case in point? The retailer is currently hosting a...

item.title: Motorola’s Razr Ultra 2026 might be a hair thicker than last year’s version
item.description: While most flagship phones have been steadily getting slimmer over recent years, the upcoming 2026 version of the Motorola Razr Ultra could end up being a bit chunkier than its predecessor. Leaked...

item.title: Open VSX Bug Let Malicious VS Code Extensions Bypass Pre-Publish Security Checks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched bug impacting Open VSX's pre-publish scanning pipeline to cause the tool to allow a malicious Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS...
